message: bd-db300.10.5: prove record decode cache wins

 {"id":"bd-db300.10.2","title":"J2: add statement and transaction-local arenas or lookaside storage for parser, VDBE, and materialization scratch","description":"## Goal
Reduce general-allocator churn on the hot execution path.

## Scope
- adopt explicit statement/transaction-local arenas or lookaside storage for parser objects, VDBE frame scratch, row materialization scratch, semantic/structural intent scratch where applicable, and other small transient buffers
- make ownership and reset boundaries explicit at reset/commit/rollback
- prefer wholesale arena drop/reset at lifecycle boundaries over repeated general-allocator churn for small transient objects
- add unit tests for reset safety, reuse safety, and no-leak/no-stale-data semantics
- add benchmark evidence showing lower allocation pressure and improved c1/mixed rows

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the targeted scratch/work objects move off the general allocator hot path
- reset/rollback semantics are explicit and covered by tests
- focused benchmarks show materially reduced allocation churn and improved baseline rows","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the targeted scratch/work objects move off the general allocator hot path
- reset/rollback semantics are explicit and covered by tests
- focused benchmarks show materially reduced allocation churn and improved baseline rows","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for arena/reset boundaries, rollback or abort cleanup, stale-data prevention, and nested statement/transaction lifecycle interactions
- add focused e2e runs that exercise repeated statements and mixed workloads with logs for arena_alloc_bytes, arena_reset_count, and fallback_to_general_allocator_count","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:57.815990752Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T11:35:30Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T11:35:30Z","close_reason":"Added statement-lookaside reuse/counters across parser and direct-insert scratch, covered reset/reuse safety with targeted core tests, and captured hot-path evidence exposing arena_alloc_bytes, arena_reset_count, and fallback_to_general_allocator_count in emitted artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","baseline-tax","db300-program","db300-track-j","memory-management","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:57.815990752Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:16:44.125022935Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2303,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.1 and O.6.
- This is the arena/lookaside bead for removing general-allocator churn from parser/VDBE/materialization hot paths.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.3","title":"J3: add reusable page-image buffers and page-buffer pools for hot staging paths","description":"## Goal
Stop paying fresh allocation and ownership churn for common page-image staging paths.

## Scope
- introduce reusable page-image storage where safe, including per-thread or per-transaction page-image slabs when evidence supports them
- reduce repeated allocation and teardown of transient page buffers on hot write/read staging paths
- prefer reusable 4KB-class buffers and COW-friendly page-image reuse where possible rather than always allocating fresh owned images
- keep pool ownership and cross-thread safety explicit
- add tests for pool reuse, reset, contention safety, and no cross-transaction contamination

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- hot page staging uses reusable buffers or pools instead of repeated fresh allocation where safe
- tests prove no stale-data leakage or lifetime confusion
- representative rows show lower allocation/copy signatures than the baseline","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- hot page staging uses reusable buffers or pools instead of repeated fresh allocation where safe
- tests prove no stale-data leakage or lifetime confusion
- representative rows show lower allocation/copy signatures than the baseline","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for buffer reuse, reset-on-recycle, contention safety, no cross-transaction contamination, and any COW-friendly reuse boundary that is introduced
- add focused e2e or microbench runs that emit page_buffer_pool_hit, page_buffer_pool_miss, staged_page_copy_bytes, and page_image_slab_reuse_count before/after data
- keep logs specific enough that future PageData/data-motion work can reuse the same evidence shape
- closure notes should state whether the chosen design is per-thread, per-transaction, pooled across lanes, or a mixture, and why that balance was chosen","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:08.876644780Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T12:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T12:18:00Z","close_reason":"Closed on the existing shared page-buffer pool implementation plus new hot-path proof surfacing: page_buffer_pool_hit/page_buffer_pool_miss, page_image_slab_reuse_count, and staged_page_copy_bytes now appear in profile.json, subsystem_profile.json, summary.md, and the baseline reuse ledger, while pager reuse and artifact smoke tests pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","baseline-tax","db300-program","db300-track-j","page-buffers","pager","track-j"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:08.876644780Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:16:54.018684455Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2304,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.2 and O.6.
- This is the reusable page-buffer/page-image-pool bead, including slab/COW-friendly reuse variants.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.4","title":"J4: implement prepared-statement and execution-plan caching with explicit invalidation","description":"## Goal
Avoid re-parsing and re-planning repeated benchmark SQL where reuse is semantically safe.

## Scope
- add a statement/plan cache with explicit invalidation on schema or semantic changes
- support reset/rebind style reuse rather than rebuild-heavy lifecycles for repeated SQL strings
- include tests for invalidation, reuse, and correctness under repeated execution and schema churn
- emit logs and metrics showing cache hit rates and the share of statements served from reuse

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- repeated SQL can be served from a cached prepared artifact when valid
- invalidation semantics are explicit and tested
- focused benchmark rows show real hit rates and throughput improvements rather than speculative value","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- repeated SQL can be served from a cached prepared artifact when valid
- invalidation semantics are explicit and tested
- focused benchmark rows show real hit rates and throughput improvements rather than speculative value","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for schema invalidation, repeated execution reuse, parameter rebinding, and cache eviction boundaries
- add focused repeated-statement e2e runs that emit statement_cache_hit, statement_cache_miss, plan_cache_hit, invalidation_reason, and compile_reuse_count
- preserve correctness-first behavior: any case that cannot be reused safely must surface as an explicit miss reason, not hidden reuse","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:19.630882183Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T11:50:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T11:50:00Z","close_reason":"Promoted the existing prepared/compiled cache implementation to closure with explicit artifact surfacing for parser reuse and compile_reuse_count in hot-path reports, while retaining the existing connection.rs invalidation and reuse regression coverage for schema churn, repeated execution, parameter rebinding, and cache boundaries","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","cache","db300-program","db300-track-j","planner","prepared-statements","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:19.630882183Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:04.034269370Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2305,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.3 and O.6.
- This is the prepared-statement / execution-plan cache bead with explicit invalidation semantics.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:38Z"},{"id":2482,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-19 Codex/FuchsiaSnow in-progress slice: cached schema-stable prepared UPDATE/DELETE direct-dispatch eligibility at prepare time in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs, leaving only the dynamic PRAGMA foreign_keys gate at execute time. Added coverage for reusable UPDATE/DELETE fast-path metadata and a prepared DELETE foreign_keys OFF->ON toggle regression. Tracker note: br update still rejects claiming this parent/child item with VALIDATION_FAILED / cannot claim blocked issue: bd-db300.10, so status could not be moved through normal claim flow.","created_at":"2026-03-19T18:03:55Z"},{"id":2483,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"2026-03-19 verification update: rch cargo check -p fsqlite-core --lib passed on the current source; rch cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib test_prepare_update_with_params -- --nocapture passed after the prepare-time fast-path metadata change. Local rustfmt --check and git diff --check are clean. A cold-worker run of test_prepared_delete_fast_path_rechecks_foreign_key_pragma_dynamically is still in flight; the code/test changes for that regression are already in the tree.","created_at":"2026-03-19T18:08:48Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.10.5","title":"J5: add cursor-level record decode caching with explicit invalidation on page or image change","description":"## Goal
Stop repeatedly decoding the same row or cell representation when the underlying page image has not changed.

## Scope
- cache decoded record or row representations at the cursor level where safe
- invalidate on page/image movement or mutation
- add unit tests for cache hit/miss correctness and invalidation on write paths
- add mixed-row and c1 evidence showing fewer record decodes per returned row

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- decode-cache hits are observable in logs and metrics on representative workloads
- invalidation semantics are correct and covered by unit tests and mixed-workload e2e checks
- representative rows show materially fewer decodes and less decode-related cost","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- decode-cache hits are observable in logs and metrics on representative workloads
- invalidation semantics are correct and covered by unit tests and mixed-workload e2e checks
- representative rows show materially fewer decodes and less decode-related cost","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for decode-cache hit/miss correctness, invalidation on page/image movement, and invalidation on write paths
- add mixed-row and c1 runs that emit decode_cache_hit, decode_cache_miss, decode_invalidation_reason, and record_decodes_per_row
- ensure future agents can distinguish true decode wins from coincidental workload changes by artifact comparison alone","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:30.234087910Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:19.937393855Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","cache","db300-program","db300-track-j","decoding","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:30.234087910Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:13.949971351Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2306,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.4 and O.6.
- This is the cursor-level record decode cache bead.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:39Z"}]}

- add mixed-row and c1 evidence showing fewer record decodes per returned row

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:

Existing VDBE engine tests already cover hit/miss and invalidation semantics, the new fsqlite-e2e repeated-column c1 probes now emit decode_cache_hit/miss plus invalidation reasons and record_decodes_per_row for both single-row and multi-row scans, the hot-path artifact report publishes a Record Decode section with those counters, and targeted rch tests passed with one decode miss per returned row while only position invalidations appear during row-to-row cursor movement.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","cache","db300-program","db300-track-j","decoding","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:30.234087910Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:13.949971351Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2306,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:

 {"id":"bd-db300.10.6","title":"J6: audit and reduce PageData ownership transitions, full-page normalization, and clone-heavy representation flips","description":"## Goal
Remove representation churn that turns ordinary page movement into clone-heavy work.

## Scope
- inventory clone->vec->arc or equivalent ownership flips in the hot path
- reduce unnecessary full-page normalization before dirtiness or mutation is known
- keep representation changes explicit and justified when they remain necessary
- add focused tests and measurements proving the hot path performs fewer ownership transitions and less byte copying

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the hot path performs fewer PageData ownership transitions and fewer unnecessary full-page normalizations
- tests cover correctness of the remaining conversion boundaries
- focused evidence shows lower memcpy/memmove/copy signatures on representative rows","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the hot path performs fewer PageData ownership transitions and fewer unnecessary full-page normalizations
- tests cover correctness of the remaining conversion boundaries
- focused evidence shows lower memcpy/memmove/copy signatures on representative rows","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add focused tests around remaining representation-conversion boundaries so ownership simplification does not silently corrupt aliasing or lifetime assumptions
- add evidence packs that report pagedata_transition_count, full_page_normalization_count, and staged_copy_bytes on representative rows
- tie any remaining expensive conversions to an explicit justification in the bead closeout","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:40.839040838Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T13:28:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T13:28:00Z","close_reason":"Closed with owned PageData short-write reuse instead of allocate-copy normalization: PageData::try_zero_extend_owned_to keeps owned buffers in place, VDBE page-data metrics/reporting now distinguish passthrough vs in-place zero-extend vs resized-copy, targeted types/vdbe/e2e proofs passed via rch, and a representative mixed_read_write c4 hot-profile run on parallel_wal_staging stayed at page_data_normalization_bytes_total=0 with 146 owned passthrough writes while the targeted short-write regression proved the in-place zero-extend lane increments without payload-copy bytes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-j","pagedata","pager","track-j"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:40.839040838Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:25.578635988Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:35.663600604Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2307,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.5 and O.6.
- This is the PageData ownership-transition / full-page-normalization reduction bead.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:41Z"},{"id":2458,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"TealStream","text":"PageData/baseline-tax note (2026-03-18):
- The prior instrumentation pass already landed PageData motion counters (`owned_passthrough`, resized copies, normalized payload bytes, zero-fill bytes) in the hot-path report pipeline. That work is the current measurement wedge for J6/J3.
- Fresh canonical c8 artifact capture with those counters is temporarily blocked by the `rch` 300s timeout on `release-perf` hot-profile replay, so there is not yet a new scored artifact pack tying PageData motion share to the current tree state.
- In parallel, I took a different bounded fixed-cost cut in `begin_concurrent.rs`: replace scan-based `release_all(txn_id)` with exact `release_set(...)` on the concurrent path when held-lock state is available. This is not a J6 change directly, but it helps separate MVCC coordination tax from PageData-motion tax so later J6 decisions are based on cleaner attribution rather than a blended microtransaction penalty.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:07:00Z"},{"id":2461,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.6","author":"TealStream","text":"Progress update (2026-03-18, pass 2):
- I re-read the landed PageData motion instrumentation in `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs` and the corresponding hot-path report wiring in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/perf_runner.rs` / `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/realdb_e2e.rs`. I did not find a concrete logic bug in the counters themselves during this pass.
- Fresh-eyes clarification: the new PageData motion report is allocator/copy evidence, not a standalone wall-time partition. The markdown/report wording already notes that component shares may overlap, so I left the semantics intact.
- This lane still needs a fresh artifact pack to become decisive, but the ranking/reporting plumbing now exposes owned passthrough vs resized-copy outcomes and total normalized bytes, which is the right evidence surface for J6/J3 once the remote replay blocker is gone.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.7","title":"J7: simplify copy-heavy decode and cell-assembly kernels while reusing cursor and frame structures","description":"## Goal
Target the measured memcpy/memmove-heavy kernels rather than making generic promises about speed.

## Scope
- isolate one or more measured copy-heavy decode/cell-assembly kernels
- simplify them or replace them with reuse-friendly kernels grounded in evidence rather than cleverness for its own sake
- reuse cursor/frame structures where that reduces setup/teardown churn without confusing lifetime boundaries
- add unit and microbenchmark tests that prove semantic parity and reduced copy volume

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- one or more measured copy-heavy kernels are materially cheaper after the change
- reused cursor/frame structures preserve correctness and are covered by tests
- benchmarks show lower copy volume or better throughput on the rows that motivated the change","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- one or more measured copy-heavy kernels are materially cheaper after the change
- reused cursor/frame structures preserve correctness and are covered by tests
- benchmarks show lower copy volume or better throughput on the rows that motivated the change","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit or microbench tests for the exact decode/cell-assembly kernels being simplified, with semantic parity assertions and copy-volume reporting
- add e2e evidence showing the targeted workloads actually exercised the optimized kernel path
- emit structured counters for hot_kernel_path_selected and kernel_copy_bytes so attribution remains concrete","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:52.137856704Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T23:29:22.309299234Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","btree","copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-j","decoding","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:10:52.137856704Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:45.778182684Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:17:56.221476593Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2308,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Primarily covers the measured-copy-reduction side of P5.6 and supports P5.9 by attacking setup/teardown and copy-heavy kernels directly.
- This bead exists because the gospel plan names reusable frames/VDBE fast paths, but the actual kernel-level copy churn also needed a first-class implementation target.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:49Z"},{"id":2469,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.7","author":"Codex","text":"In progress: narrowed the direct table-program execution path so runtime metadata is scoped per statement instead of rebuilding blanket insert/default/autoincrement inputs every execution. Simple VALUES/DEFAULT VALUES inserts now skip the connection-level legacy default-map pass, direct insert paths scope AUTOINCREMENT sequence lookup to the target root page only, and the hot-path profile artifacts now expose prepared-engine reuse plus connection-ceremony counters (prepared engine fresh/reuse, snapshot reuse/parks, schema/publication refreshes, sqlite_sequence fast-path vs scan fallback). Targeted core/e2e verification is running via rch.","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:29:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.10.8","title":"J8: add VDBE fast paths, reusable execution frames, and safe direct read-path improvements where profitable","description":"## Goal
Shrink non-structural execution overhead in the statement engine and read path.

## Scope
- add evidence-driven VDBE fast paths or fused hot opcode handling where profitable and maintainable
- reuse execution frames/cursor scaffolding where safe
- evaluate safe direct pager-buffer fills, registered read buffers, optional mmap-like read paths, or equivalent read-path reductions for benchmarked environments without violating correctness or portability constraints
- add tests that prove semantic parity and logs that make fast-path selection visible

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- fast paths are evidence-driven and visibly exercised in representative workloads
- execution-frame reuse and read-path improvements preserve correctness under test
- representative c1 or mixed rows improve for reasons that can be traced back to the new fast paths","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- fast paths are evidence-driven and visibly exercised in representative workloads
- execution-frame reuse and read-path improvements preserve correctness under test
- representative c1 or mixed rows improve for reasons that can be traced back to the new fast paths","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for fast-path correctness, frame reuse safety, direct read-path boundary conditions, and any registered-buffer or mmap-like path boundary that is introduced
- add representative c1/mixed runs that emit vdbe_fast_path_selected, frame_reuse_hit, direct_read_path_used, registered_read_buffer_used, mmap_like_path_used, and fast_path_fallback_reason
- keep any fast path behind explicit observability so future regressions can be localized quickly
- optimize for users by preferring read-path improvements that remain understandable, optional where needed, and clearly bounded by environment/portability assumptions","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaSnow","owner":"FuchsiaSnow","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:02.906268102Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-19T05:12:15.233407283Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline-tax","db300-program","db300-track-j","read-path","track-j","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:11:02.906268102Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:18:06.622932299Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:18:16.942865773Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2309,"issue_id":"bd-db300.10.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:
- Directly covers P5.6, P5.7, and O.6.
- This is the VDBE fast-path / reusable-frame / safe direct-read-path bead, including registered-buffer and optional mmap-like explorations where justified.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:44:50Z"}]}

@@ -7794,6 +7794,7 @@ mod tests {
                 vdbe_decoded_value_heap_bytes_total: 16,
                 decode_cache_hits_total: 0,
                 decode_cache_misses_total: 1,
+                record_decodes_per_row_basis_points: Some(10_000),
                 decode_cache_invalidations_position_total: 0,
                 decode_cache_invalidations_write_total: 0,
                 decode_cache_invalidations_pseudo_total: 0,
